title: Debugging, Review, and Adversarial Prompting · Contract Clinic: Review Frame
summary: Diagnose a failing build and review the repair against a base branch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt review frame increment in an evidence-first debug and review playbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define review baseline, risk categories, and evidence required for actionable findings.
Prompt for the smallest cause-level repair and a regression test.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Review Frame
steps.instruction:
theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt debug review that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Review Frame contract for the supplied data.

content.text: Use a changed theory case for prompt debug review that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Review Frame condition decided your prediction.

options.text: For predict review frame, apply Review Frame without its stated preconditions, omit the an evidence-first debug and review playbook trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Review Frame for an evidence-first debug and review playbook.
Define review baseline, risk categories, and evidence required for actionable findings. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Review Frame

theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt debug review that tests this competency: Define review baseline, risk categories, and evidence required for actionable findings.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Define review baseline, risk categories, and evidence required for actionable findings. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check the Review Frame preconditions, operate on an evidence-first debug and review playbook in an inspectable order, then verify its result and boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case for prompt debug review that tests this competency: Define review baseline, risk categories, and evidence required for actionable findings.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Review Frame, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an evidence-first debug and review playbook.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A code review should list every stylistic preference. This fails the Review Frame contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Review Frame evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-review-frame-1t9rob" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Review Frame into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-review-frame-1t9rob
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
